title: Red and White Freedom Flight
story: Every August 17, Indonesia celebrates its independence with amazing traditions! People hang red and white flags everywhere and compete in fun games called lomba. Kids race while balancing eggs on spoons, climb greasy poles, and enter sack races. Families feast together and watch parades filled with colorful costumes and music that fills the streets with joy.
activity_question: What are the traditional games called that Indonesians play on Independence Day?
follow_up_question: What other creative games could you invent for a independence celebration?

title: Treasure Hunting at Thrift Shops
story: Every August 17, bargain hunters celebrate National Thrift Shop Day by digging through secondhand stores for amazing finds! People love searching through racks of vintage clothes, old books, quirky decorations, and forgotten toys. You might discover a cool jacket from the 1980s, a rare comic book, or even a board game missing just one piece. Thrift shopping helps the environment by giving old items new homes instead of throwing them away. Many shoppers share their best discoveries on social media with hashtags like #ThriftFind. It's like a treasure hunt where you never know what gems you'll uncover!
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Thrift Shop Day?
follow_up_question: What's the coolest thing you think you could find at a thrift shop?

title: Custard's Last Stand: National Vanilla Custard Day
story: Spoons everywhere celebrate on August 17 for National Vanilla Custard Day! This creamy, dreamy dessert gets its own special day when bakeries showcase their fluffiest custard creations. People share photos of custard tarts, éclairs, and bowls of homemade vanilla custard online. Some families make custard from scratch, stirring eggs and cream until perfectly smooth. Ice cream shops often feature vanilla custard flavors, and restaurants add custard desserts to their menus for the day.
activity_question: What do people often share online on National Vanilla Custard Day?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite way to eat vanilla custard - in a tart, éclair, or plain bowl?

title: Black Cats Get Their Purr-fect Day
story: Black cats strut their stuff on August 17 during National Black Cat Appreciation Day! This special holiday helps people see how amazing these sleek, beautiful cats really are. Many folks still think black cats bring bad luck, but cat lovers know better. On this day, animal shelters share photos of adorable black cats looking for homes, and people post pictures of their own black kitties on social media. Some families even adopt black cats on August 17 to give them the love they deserve. It's the perfect time to learn that black cats are just as sweet, playful, and cuddly as any other cat!
activity_question: What do animal shelters often do on National Black Cat Appreciation Day?
activity_choices: Share photos of black cats looking for homes
Paint all cats black for the day
Only allow black cats to play outside
Give black cats special black food
activity_answer: Share photos of black cats looking for homes
bonus_fact: Black cats are actually considered good luck in many countries around the world!

title: Put Your Best Foot Forward Day!
story: Your feet carry you everywhere, and on August 17, it's time to show them some love! National I Love My Feet Day encourages people to pamper their hardworking toes and soles. Many folks celebrate by getting pedicures, soaking their feet in warm water, or buying comfy new shoes. Some people share photos of their colorful nail polish or funny socks on social media.
activity_question: How do many people pamper their feet on National I Love My Feet Day?
activity_choices: Getting pedicures and soaking them in warm water
activity_answer: Getting pedicures and soaking them in warm water
bonus_fact: Your feet have over 250,000 sweat glands - more than anywhere else on your body!

title: Heroes Without Paychecks Day
story: Every August 17, National Nonprofit Day celebrates organizations that help others without trying to make money. These groups feed hungry families, protect animals, clean up parks, and support kids in need. People share stories about their favorite nonprofits on social media and volunteer their time to help out. It's a perfect day to discover amazing groups doing good work in your community!
activity_question: What do nonprofit organizations try to do?
activity_choices: Make as much money as possible
Help others without trying to make money
activity_answer: Help others without trying to make money
follow_up_question: What cause would you want to help if you started your own nonprofit?
